findings.summary: Component counts, BOM, and power rails correctly parsed across 4 sheets; VGA15 connector (VGA1) misclassified as varistor; Active oscillator Q1 reports output_net as 'GND' instead of the actual clo...

correct.description: 239 components across main + 3 sub-sheets, power rails +3.3V/+5V/+5V_USB/GND/PWR_FLAG/VBAT all found, diode count (58 TVS + misc), crystal Q3 at 12MHz with 27pF load caps correctly identified.
differential_pairs contains D+/D- with shared_ics=['U6'] (USBLC6-2P6), has_esd=true. The USB ESD protection IC is correctly identified.

MICROSD SPI bus found with MISO net and chip select. PSRAM signals PSRAM_SIO1/SIO2/SIO3/PSRAM_SCLK/PSRAM_CE between U3 (ESP32) and U4 (APS6404L PSRAM) captured in multi_ic_nets. Memory interface U4 correctly linked to U3.

incorrect.description: VGA1 has value 'VGA15' and footprint 'VGA15-F(CD0515S21J0)'. The analyzer classified it as type 'varistor' in both the BOM and protection_devices. The 'VGA15' value string is being matched against a varistor pattern. VGA1 is a standard 15-pin D-sub VGA output connector and should be type 'connector'. Same issue in Rev B.

Q1 (ASE-18.432MHZ-L-C-T) is a 4-pin active oscillator. Tracing pin connections in the schematic: the output pin wires from 5300,3900 up through 5200,3900→5200,3700→5200,3400, then connects to the EZ80 GPIO_PD7 net via wire at 4200,3400. The analyzer reports output_net='GND' which is incorrect — the output net should be the GPIO_PD7/EZ80 CLK input net. The GND is the oscillator's power ground pin, not the output. Same error in Rev B.

The bus_analysis.i2c section contains an entry with net='GND', line='SDA', listing 18 devices including U1 through U8/U9/U10/U6. The pull-ups R9 and R10 (4.7K to +3.3V) are correctly identified as I2C pull-ups, but they appear to connect to EZ80 pins on the SDA signal — not the GND net. The EZ80 ZDI (Z80 Debug Interface) uses ZDA/ZCLK which may match SDA/SCL patterns. The GND net entry is a false positive contaminating real I2C data. Also RST\EN net is classified as SCL line (also false positive).

The bridge_circuits analysis reports FET1 and FET2 (BSS138 N-MOSFETs) as a half-bridge with high_gate=KCLK, low_gate=KDAT, and the ESP32 (U3) as the driver IC. In reality, these BSS138 MOSFETs are used as PS/2 keyboard open-drain level shifters for the KDAT and KCLK lines — not a motor/power half-bridge. The output_net, power_net, and ground_net are all reported as '+3.3V' which is nonsensical for a bridge. Same false positive in Rev B.

findings.summary: Rev B shares all three incorrect findings from Rev A (VGA1 as varistor, Q1 output_net=GND, FET1/FET2 half-bridge)

incorrect.description: All three misclassifications confirmed present in the Rev B output: VGA1 type='varistor' in BOM and protection_devices; Q1 active_oscillator output_net='GND'; FET1/FET2 half_bridge topology with KCLK/KDAT gates. The designs are substantially similar so the same analyzer bugs trigger on both revisions.
